text: On Tuesday, the World Council of Orthodox Jewish Communities, a Hasidic organization, issued a rabbinical court summons to the World Jewish Restitution Organization (WJRO), chaired by Bronfman, according to Rabbi Chaim Stauber, the president of the World Council.

Within the summons, referred to as a "beit din," the World Council's members accuse Bronfman of "misrepresenting" the Jewish community and insist that he cease any ongoing negotiations until their concerns have been resolved.
However, the World Council, which is chiefly comprised of individuals who survived pre-war European Jewry, complain that their efforts to get restitution claims addressed have not succeeded.
They are forbidden from suing fellow Jews in a state court, and must instead seek to have their disputes addressed by a panel of three rabbis in a beit din.

Different segments of the Jewish community remain united in their efforts to recover missing assets from Switzerland and other locations, yet they are often divided over the best way to do it and how to distribute the recovered funds once obtained.
Although the World Council represents thousands of Hasidic Jews, it has remained separate from the sphere of more conventional Jewish organizations.

Democrats have repeatedly complained that the South American accords — along with various other agreements signed during the Bush administration — skimp when it comes to labor rights protections.

Interview: Africa seeks aid in adjusting to climate change

African nations require financing, the Clean Development Mechanism (CDM), and technology transfer to combat climate change, Kivutha Kibwana, president-designate of the UN Climate Change Conference has said.
They are impacted to a considerable extent due to the climate change.
As a result, they are frustrated because they cannot mobilize the funds that are required for specific adaptation during this process.
Three funds have been established within the Climate Change Convention and Kyoto Protocol to help developing countries.

Within these, the Adaptation Fund, which will be discussed during the Nairobi meeting to reach consensus on how it should be managed, is designated to finance practical adaptation projects and initiatives in developing nations and support activities for capacity-building.

California Governor Arnold Schwarzenegger was expected to decide on Monday if he will spare former gang boss Stanley "Tookie" Williams, whose execution is scheduled to be executed in the early hours of Tuesday.
Williams, whose situation has prompted an international campaign for clemency, faces execution for murdering four individuals unless the governor intervenes prior to Tuesday.

His attorneys argued that he experienced a change of heart displayed by his work to turn individuals away from gang violence and asked that his death sentence be commuted to life imprisonment without the possibility of parole.
The creator of the infamous Crips gang in Los Angeles, who has appealed to young people to avoid gangs, has been nominated on multiple occasions for the Nobel Peace Prize.

India confront pace barrage in warm-up match




India will have the advantage of playing on one of the finest batting surfaces in South Africa as they warm up for a Test series versus the host side - but they will also have to deal with yet another group of fast bowlers.

The Rest of South Africa lineup which faces the Indians in a four-day fixture starting Thursday features Mornantau Hayward, regarded as one of the quickest bowlers in the nation, along with Morne Morkel, an emerging talent who has been tipped for Test recognition shortly, as well as Garnett Kruger, who has hovered on the edges of the national squad over the last two seasons.
The Indians go into the warm-up match lacking in confidence after being beaten 4-0 in a one-day series where they failed to deal effectively with the South African pace attack.

As long as they are able to weather the initial spells from the quick bowlers, this could serve as perfect preparation for the visiting side, who play the opening Test of their three-match series against South Africa in Johannesburg between December 15 and 19.
